channel.title: VPS Reviews on VPS Catalog - Find the Best VPS Hosting for Your Needs
channel.description: Recent content in VPS Reviews on VPS Catalog - Find the Best VPS Hosting for Your Needs

item.title: Cloudways Review 2026
item.description:
Overview

Cloudways isn’t a hosting provider — it’s a managed platform that runs on top of DigitalOcean, Vultr, Linode, AWS, and Google Cloud. You pay for the underlying server + Cloudways’ management fee.

Pricing

Plan Server Provider RAM Storage Bandwidth Monthly Cost
DigitalOcean $11/mo DO 1 GB 25 GB 1 TB $11/mo
DigitalOcean $23/mo DO 2 GB 50 GB 2 TB $23/mo
Vultr $14/mo Vultr 1 GB 25 GB 1 TB $14/mo

Verdict

Cloudways is excellent if you want managed VPS without learning server administration. The 24/7 support actually knows what they’re doing.

item.title: Contabo Review 2026
item.description:
Overview

Contabo is the spec king. At €5.99/mo you get 4 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM — competitors charge $12-24/mo for similar specs. The trade-off is network consistency.

Performance (€5.99/mo Plan)

  • Geekbench 6: 1,432 (single-core)
  • Disk I/O: 1.4 GB/s read, 620 MB/s write
  • Network: High variance (peak 800 Mbps, off-peak 300 Mbps)
  • Uptime (30 days): 99.92%

Verdict

Contabo is a great pick if you need raw CPU and RAM on a tight budget. The network inconsistency means it’s not ideal for latency-sensitive applications.

item.title: DigitalOcean Review 2026
item.description:
Overview

DigitalOcean has been the go-to VPS for developers since 2012. The experience is polished — from signup to deploying your first droplet takes less than 2 minutes.

Performance (Premium $14/mo Droplet)

  • Geekbench 6: 1,245 (single-core)
  • Disk I/O: 1.8 GB/s read (NVMe), 720 MB/s write
  • Network: 980 Mbps download, 920 Mbps upload
  • Uptime (30 days): 99.99%

Verdict

If you’re a developer or team that values a polished experience and great documentation, DigitalOcean is worth the slight premium over budget providers.

item.title: Hetzner Review 2026
item.description:
Overview

Hetzner is a German hosting company that’s been around since 1997. Their cloud VPS line launched a few years ago and immediately shook up the market with aggressive pricing.

Performance (CX32 — 2 vCPU, 8 GB, €6.99/mo)

  • Geekbench 6: 1,856 (single-core)
  • Disk I/O: 2.1 GB/s read (NVMe), 870 MB/s write
  • Network: 1.8 Gbps download, 1.2 Gbps upload
  • Uptime (30 days): 99.97%

The CX32 outperforms most $20/mo plans from other providers.

item.title: Linode Review 2026
item.description:
Overview

Linode (acquired by Akamai in 2022) has long been a developer favorite. The documentation is still best-in-class, but the acquisition has brought some changes — some good, some not.

Performance ($5/mo Plan)

  • Geekbench 6: 1,089 (single-core)
  • Disk I/O: 950 MB/s read, 510 MB/s write
  • Network: 910 Mbps download, 840 Mbps upload
  • Uptime (30 days): 99.96%

Verdict

Still a solid choice for beginners who want great documentation. But the post-acquisition support dip means you might prefer DigitalOcean for similar pricing.

item.title: RackNerd Review 2026
item.description:
Overview

RackNerd has grown rapidly in the budget VPS space. Their promotional pricing is aggressive, and for the price, you get decent specs.

Verdict

A good budget option for US-based projects. The lack of live chat support is frustrating when you need quick help. If you need European hosting, look elsewhere.

Visit RackNerd →

item.title: Vultr Review 2026
item.description:
Overview

Vultr is one of the most popular VPS providers, and for good reason. With 32 data centers worldwide and prices starting at $2.50/mo, it’s a strong contender for budget-conscious users who need global reach.

Performance

I ran a series of benchmarks on Vultr’s $6/mo plan (1 vCPU, 1 GB RAM, 25 GB SSD):

  • Geekbench 6: 1,012 (single-core)
  • Disk I/O: 1.2 GB/s read, 580 MB/s write
  • Network: 950 Mbps download, 850 Mbps upload (US East)
  • Uptime (30 days): 99.98%

Pricing Breakdown

Plan vCPU RAM Storage Bandwidth Price
Regular 1 512 MB 10 GB 500 GB $2.50/mo
Regular 1 1 GB 25 GB 1 TB $6/mo
Regular 2 2 GB 55 GB 2 TB $12/mo
Regular 4 4 GB 80 GB 3 TB $24/mo
High Freq 1 1 GB 32 GB 1 TB $7/mo
High Freq 2 2 GB 64 GB 2 TB $14/mo

Verdict

Vultr is an excellent choice if you need global coverage or a budget-friendly entry point. It’s not the most polished experience, but the raw performance and pricing are solid.
